zakariya_prayer.title: The prayer of Zakariya
zakariya_prayer.introduction: Zakariya (زكريا عليه السلام), Zechariah, is a prophet and priest of the Children of Israel. The Quran says: "It was then that Zakariya invoked his Lord." (3:38) Zakariya was old and had no children. His wife was barren. Despite this, he did not despair. He invoked his Lord in secret.
zakariya_prayer.conclusion: Zakariya asked for a pious heir. He wanted someone to perpetuate Allah's message after him. His prayer was sincere and selfless. Allah answered it.
zakariya_prayer.moral_lesson: This story teaches us that sincere invocation reaches Allah. Even when everything seems impossible, the believer must hope in his Lord's mercy.

chapters.title: The desire for a son
chapters.content: The Quran relates: "My Lord! Grant me from Yourself a good offspring. You are the Best of inheritors." (19:5-6) Zakariya served in the Temple. He saw Mary, the mother of Isa, receiving her sustenance from Allah. This encouraged him. He invoked in secret: "Give me a pious son." He knew that Allah can do anything.

chapters.title: The secret invocation
chapters.content: The Quran says: "He invoked his Lord in secret. He said: My Lord! My bones have weakened and my head has flamed with white hair. And I have never been unhappy in invoking You." (19:3-4) Zakariya invoked with humility. He acknowledged his weakness. He reminded Allah that he had always served Him. His invocation was answered.

references.content: هُنَالِكَ دَعَا زَكَرِيَّا رَبَّهُۥ ۖ قَالَ رَبِّ هَبْ لِى مِن لَّدُنكَ ذُرِّيَّةًۭ طَيِّبَةً ۖ إِنَّكَ سَمِيعُ ٱلدُّعَآءِ
references.translation: It was then that Zakariya invoked his Lord. He said: My Lord! Grant me from Your presence a good offspring. You are the One who hears invocation.

references.content: ذِكْرُ رَحْمَتِ رَبِّكَ عَبْدَهُۥ زَكَرِيَّآ ۝ إِذْ نَادَىٰ رَبَّهُۥ نِدَآءً خَفِيًّۭا ۝ قَالَ رَبِّ إِنِّى وَهَنَ ٱلْعَظْمُ مِنِّى وَٱشْتَعَلَ ٱلرَّأْسُ شَيْبًۭا
references.translation: Remembrance of your Lord's mercy towards His servant Zakariya. When he invoked his Lord in secret.
references.relevance: This passage describes Zakariya's secret invocation.

glossary.definition: Zechariah in Arabic. Prophet and father of Yahya.
Invocation. Zakariya made du'a for a son.

zakariya_announcement.title: The announcement to Zakariya
zakariya_announcement.introduction: Allah answered Zakariya's invocation. The Quran says: "O Zakariya! We give you good news of a son. His name will be Yahya." (19:7) Angels came to Zakariya while he was praying in the sanctuary. They announced the birth of a son. Zakariya asked for a sign.
zakariya_announcement.conclusion: Allah gave him a sign: he would not be able to speak to people for three nights. This was proof that the promise was true. Zakariya left the sanctuary and signaled to his people to glorify Allah.
zakariya_announcement.moral_lesson: This story teaches us that Allah answers the invocations of the pious. Miracles confirm the truth of the prophetic message.

chapters.title: The good news
chapters.content: The Quran relates: "O Zakariya! We give you good news of a son. His name will be Yahya. We have not given before anyone of that name." (19:7) Zakariya was in the mihrab (sanctuary) when he heard the announcement. He could not believe it: him, old, and his wife barren? But Allah said to him: "So it is. Your Lord has said: That is easy for Me."

chapters.title: The sign
chapters.content: The Quran says: "He said: My Lord! How can I have a son when my wife is barren and I have reached extreme old age? He said: So it is. Your Lord has said: That is easy for Me. And indeed I created you before, when you were nothing." (19:8-9) Zakariya asked for a sign. Allah said: "Your sign will be that you will not speak to people for three nights while being in good health." (19:10)

references.content: يَـٰزَكَرِيَّآ إِنَّا نُبَشِّرُكَ بِغُلَـٰمٍ ٱسْمُهُۥ يَحْيَىٰ لَمْ نَجْعَل لَّهُۥ مِن قَبْلُ سَمِيًّۭا
references.translation: O Zakariya! We give you good news of a son. His name will be Yahya.
references.relevance: This verse relates the announcement of Yahya's birth.

references.content: فَنَادَتْهُ ٱلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةُ وَهُوَ قَآئِمٌۭ يُصَلِّى فِى ٱلْمِحْرَابِ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ يُبَشِّرُكَ بِيَحْيَىٰ
references.translation: The angels called him while he was praying in the sanctuary: Allah gives you good news of Yahya.
references.relevance: This verse describes the announcement in the sanctuary.

glossary.definition: Good news. Zakariya received the bashara of a son.
Sanctuary, place of prayer. Zakariya prayed in the mihrab.

zakariya_yahya.title: Zakariya and the birth of Yahya
zakariya_yahya.introduction: Yahya was born as Allah had promised. The Quran says: "O Yahya! Take the Book with strength. And We gave him wisdom from childhood." (19:12) Zakariya could not speak for three days. When Yahya was born, Zakariya recovered his speech. He glorified Allah and thanked his Lord.
zakariya_yahya.conclusion: Yahya grew up in piety. He became a just and pure prophet. Zakariya had obtained what he desired: an heir to perpetuate Allah's message. Allah's promise had been fulfilled.
zakariya_yahya.moral_lesson: This story teaches us that Allah's word is truth. What He promises, He fulfills. The believer must have total trust in his Lord.

chapters.title: The birth of Yahya
chapters.content: The Quran relates: "And peace be upon him the day he was born, the day he dies, and the day he will be raised alive." (19:15) Yahya was born to Zakariya's wife. It was a miracle: a barren woman and an old man had a child. Family and relatives rejoiced. Zakariya signaled to his people to glorify Allah morning and evening.

chapters.title: The upbringing of Yahya
chapters.content: The Quran says: "And (Yahya) was dutiful to his parents. He was neither arrogant nor disobedient." (19:14) Zakariya raised Yahya in the worship of Allah. Yahya was a pious, gentle and obedient child. He learned the Torah and wisdom. He became a prophet even before adulthood. Zakariya had passed on the torch.

references.content: يَـٰيَحْيَىٰ خُذِ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ بِقُوَّةٍۢ ۖ وَءَاتَيْنَـٰهُ ٱلْحُكْمَ صَبِيًّۭا ۝ وَحَنَانًۭا مِّن لَّدُنَّا وَزَكَوٰةًۭ
references.translation: O Yahya! Take the Book with strength. We gave him wisdom from childhood, and tenderness from Us.

references.content: قَالَ رَبِّ ٱجْعَل لِّىٓ ءَايَةًۭ ۖ قَالَ ءَايَتُكَ أَلَّا تُكَلِّمَ ٱلنَّاسَ ثَلَـٰثَ لَيَالٍۢ سَوِيًّۭا
references.translation: He said: My Lord! Give me a sign. He said: Your sign will be that you will not speak to people for three nights.
references.relevance: This verse relates the sign given to Zakariya.

glossary.definition: John in Arabic. Son of Zakariya, prophet of purity.
Wisdom. Yahya received hikma from childhood.

lessons_from_zakariya.title: Lessons from the story of Zakariya
lessons_from_zakariya.introduction: Zakariya is the prophet of answered invocation. The Quran says: "It was then that Zakariya invoked his Lord." (3:38) An old man without children, he invoked with sincerity. Allah gave him Yahya, a pure prophet. His story teaches that nothing is impossible for Allah.
lessons_from_zakariya.conclusion: Zakariya invoked in secret. Allah answered. Yahya was born. The message was perpetuated. Zakariya's story is a reminder: sincere invocation opens the gates of heaven.
lessons_from_zakariya.moral_lesson: This story teaches us that the believer must invoke Allah in all circumstances. Even when hope seems lost, Allah's mercy can transform everything.

chapters.title: Sincere invocation
chapters.content: The Quran says: "Invoke Me, I will answer you." (40:60) Zakariya invoked with humility. He did not ask for himself a fleeting enjoyment. He asked for a pious heir to serve Allah. His intention was pure. Allah answered. Invocation must be sincere and selfless.

chapters.title: Trust in Allah
chapters.content: Zakariya asked for a sign. He did not doubt Allah, but wanted proof for his heart. Allah gave him the sign of silence. When Yahya was born, everything was confirmed. The believer can ask Allah for signs. Faith grows when we see promises fulfilled.

references.content: هُنَالِكَ دَعَا زَكَرِيَّا رَبَّهُۥ ۖ قَالَ رَبِّ هَبْ لِى مِن لَّدُنكَ ذُرِّيَّةًۭ طَيِّبَةً ۖ إِنَّكَ سَمِيعُ ٱلدُّعَآءِ
references.translation: Zakariya invoked his Lord. Grant me a good offspring. You hear invocation.
Remembrance of your Lord's mercy towards His servant Zakariya.
references.relevance: This passage relates the complete story of Zakariya and Yahya.

glossary.definition: Answer. Allah granted istijaba to Zakariya.
